descriptive_fit.honesty_note: Descriptive fit from the broader benchmark-local search winner; not an operator publication.

descriptive_fit_status.headline: Benchmark-local descriptive fit is available for this run.

portfolio_selection.selection_explanation: Winner analytic_backend / analytic_lag1_affine; 8909 total code bits; runner-up algorithmic_search_backend / algorithmic_last_observation at 8971; selected by min total code bits then max description gain then min structure code bits then min canonical byte length then candidate id

target.analysis_note: Raw close levels usually reward persistence and drift. Treat any descriptive equation on this target as a level-tracking summary, not evidence of deeper market structure.
target.description: Predict the raw close level for each trading day.
target.label: Price Close
target.recommended_reason: Raw levels are useful for replaying a published point path, but they often collapse into near-persistence equations.

descriptive_fit.honesty_note: Descriptive fit from the broader benchmark-local search winner; not an operator publication.

semantic_audit.headline: Raw close descriptive fit is effectively a persistence equation with a tiny intercept.
semantic_audit.recommended_target_reason: Better default for interpretable descriptive equations because it makes level persistence explicit instead of hiding it inside raw prices.
semantic_audit.summary: Against a naive y(t)=y(t-1) baseline, this fit only improves one-step MAE by 0.4%. Treat it as a level-tracking summary, not a meaningful structural market equation.

publication.headline: Operator replay verified a candidate, but the point lane did not clear publication gates.

search_scope.headline: Operator point lane only searched the current-release surface (constant, drift, linear_trend, seasonal_naive).

evidence.headline: Only 2 realized forecasts across 1 origins. Calibration can pass here without proving robustness.
